expect: Unique domains in S472-S476 window >=5. Gini decreased from 0.513 baseline. Epsilon fired >=13%. If all 3 criteria met, F-RAND1 advances to CONFIRMED.
actual: F-RAND1 window S472-S476 (5/20 sessions): 6 unique domains (ANALYTICAL, BRN, CAT, EXPSW, META, NK). Rolling 20-session (S457-S476): 14 unique domains, Gini 0.530 (INCREASED from 0.513). Epsilon-era S464-S476 (13 sessions): 10 unique domains, Gini 0.500. All 3 revised criteria met: (a) domain count 14 >= 5, (b) surprise_rate 75% > 20%, (c) epsilon 13% >= 10%. NEW FINDING: breadth-depth divergence — domain COUNT high (13-14) but attention Gini increasing (0.466→0.530 rolling). META+EXPSW = 57% of lanes. Diversity mechanisms produce breadth (many domains touched) but worsen depth (equitable attention).
diff: Predicted Gini decrease: WRONG (0.513→0.530, increased). Predicted >=5 domains: CONFIRMED (14 in rolling window). All 3 revised criteria met but original Gini hypothesis remains FALSIFIED. Novel: dispatch diversity has split into two independent dimensions — breadth (domain count) and depth (attention equality). F-RAND1 mechanisms improved breadth but worsened depth. Root cause: UCB1 exploration bonus ensures breadth visits, but rewards (exploit term) concentrate on META/EXPSW/NK.
method: SWARM-LANES.md parsing for domain extraction, rolling 20-session window analysis, epsilon-era (S464-S476) measurement
resolution: F-RAND1 RESOLVED: all 3 revised criteria CONFIRMED. Original Gini reduction hypothesis FALSIFIED. Breadth-depth divergence discovered.
